commands.summary: New installer/packaging tests: every local module a packaged module requires is itself in builder.json files (prevents the attention.js-style missing-module crash); test files are excluded; renderer bundle + backend + icon are packaged and source maps are not; if an unpacked build exists it contains ForgeLink.exe. 4/4 pass.

Opt-in live Twilio test is present and skipped by default; it runs validateTwilioCredentials against the real API only when FORGELINK_LIVE_TWILIO=1 and TWILIO_* are set, so CI never needs network or a real account.

All layers green: backend HTTP integration (server.test.ts), renderer interaction (App.test.tsx, 20), Electron lifecycle (lifecycle.test.js, tunnel.test.js), security (security.test.js), and the new packaging tests; the live test reports skipped. node --check covers every entry module.

environment.process_note: PR-013 test pyramid: the base (unit), middle (backend HTTP integration + renderer interaction), and lifecycle layers already existed; this adds the two missing layers - static installer/packaging completeness tests and an opt-in live Twilio test - completing the pyramid. Electron main-process integration (utilityProcess crash/restart, single-instance) remains runtime-verified rather than unit-tested because it needs a live Electron process; the pure lifecycle logic behind it is unit-tested.
